courseTitle: Elevation Plan Drawing — Municipal Building Standards
courseDescription: Complete guide to creating professional elevation plans for municipal submission. Covers exterior building faces, materials, dimensions, architectural features, field measurements, and all building code requirements for building permits and façade approval.

practicalExamples.title: Modern Residential Elevation
steps: Measure building heights using laser level or total station

practicalExamples.title: Commercial Building Elevation
steps: Design storefront and office façade elements

practicalExamples.title: Educational Institution Elevation
practicalExamples.description: School building with multiple façade treatments
steps: Plan safety features and emergency exits

courseObjectives: Create accurate elevation plans showing all building faces
Include architectural features, materials, and exterior finishes
Add proper dimensions, grades, and building code annotations
Produce municipal-compliant elevation plans ready for building permit submission

phases.phaseTitle: Setup — Elevation Plan Standards and Layers
phases.phaseDescription: Prepare drawing environment with proper units, layers, and architectural standards.

steps.title: Configure Drawing Units and Scale
steps.subtitle: Set up for elevation plan requirements
steps.why: Elevation plans typically use 1:50 or 1:100 scale and require millimeter precision for accurate construction.
instructions: Type UNITS and set Type to Decimal, Insertion Scale to Millimeters
Set Precision to 0 for whole millimeters
Set LIMITS to accommodate building width and height (e.g., 0,0 to 25000,15000 for 25m wide x 15m high)
Set annotation scale to 1:50 for detailed elevations or 1:100 for overall elevations
Save as Elevation_Plan_Template.dwt for future projects

steps.title: Create Elevation Plan Layer System
steps.subtitle: Organize all elevation elements
steps.why: Proper layer organization is essential for controlling visibility, printing different elevations, and municipal review.
instructions: Create structural layers: WALL_ELEV (Red, 0.40mm), FOUNDATION (Brown, 0.50mm), ROOF_LINE (Blue, 0.35mm)
Create opening layers: DOOR_ELEV (Magenta, 0.25mm), WINDOW_ELEV (Cyan, 0.25mm), BALCONY (Green, 0.30mm)
Create material layers: BRICK_PATTERN (Orange, 0.20mm), STONE_PATTERN (Gray, 0.20mm), STUCCO (Light Gray, 0.18mm)
Create annotation layers: TEXT_ELEV (White, 0.18mm), DIM_ELEV (Yellow, 0.18mm), HATCH_ELEV (Dark Gray, 0.13mm)
Create detail layers: MOULDING (White, 0.15mm), TRIM (Light Blue, 0.20mm), HARDWARE (Black, 0.25mm)
Create reference layers: GRADE_LINE (Brown, 0.25mm), GRID_REF (Gray, 0.09mm), EXISTING (Red, 0.25mm, Dashed)

phases.phaseDescription: Establish the building outline and structural elements.

steps.title: Create Building Outline with Field Measurements
steps.subtitle: Define building footprint using measured heights and dimensions
steps.why: Building outline must match actual field measurements and provide accurate reference for elevation details.
instructions: Set current layer to WALL_ELEV
Field Method: Measure building height using laser level or total station
Draw grade line as horizontal reference (use actual surveyed grade)
Create building outline using measured width from site
Add height verification notes: 'Measured on-site 2025-01-15'
Use OFFSET to show wall depth if required (typically 200-300mm)
Verify building height matches architectural drawings within tolerance

practicalTip.title: Height Measurement Accuracy
practicalTip.text: Measure building height at multiple points to account for slope or settlement. Use the highest point for maximum height compliance check.

steps.title: Add Foundation and Grade Line
steps.subtitle: Show ground level and foundation
steps.why: Foundation and grade line show the building's relationship to ground level.
instructions: Set current layer to FOUNDATION
Draw grade line showing existing ground level
Use OFFSET to show foundation depth below grade
Add foundation wall if visible in elevation
Hatch ground area below grade with earth pattern
Add finished floor height above grade (typically 300-600mm)

steps.title: Define Floor Levels and Ceilings
steps.subtitle: Show floor-to-floor heights
steps.why: Floor levels must be clearly shown for construction and code compliance.
instructions: Continue with WALL_ELEV layer
Draw floor lines at each level using horizontal lines
Use OFFSET to create standard floor heights (typically 3000mm)
Show ceiling lines if different from floor heights
Add floor level annotations (e.g., 'GF', 'FF', 'SF' for ground, first, second floor)
Include floor-to-floor height dimensions

phases.phaseDescription: Add doors, windows, and other openings with proper symbols.

steps.title: Draw Doors in Elevation
steps.subtitle: Show door types and details
steps.why: Doors must be properly shown with type, size, and architectural details.
instructions: Set current layer to DOOR_ELEV
Draw door opening dimensions based on floor plan
Show door frame and panel details
Add door hardware (handles, locks) if visible
Show door swing direction if applicable
Add door size annotations and type specifications

steps.title: Draw Windows and Openings
steps.subtitle: Show window types and details
steps.why: Windows are major architectural elements and must be accurately detailed.
instructions: Set current layer to WINDOW_ELEV
Draw window opening dimensions based on floor plan
Show window frame and mullion details
Add glazing lines to show window panes
Show window sill and head details
Add window size annotations and opening type if operable

steps.title: Add Special Openings and Features
steps.subtitle: Show other openings and architectural features
steps.why: Special openings like garage doors, vents, and grilles must be properly shown.
instructions: Draw garage doors with panel details
Add ventilation grilles and louvers
Show skylights or roof windows if visible
Add decorative openings or architectural features
Include mechanical equipment openings if visible

phases.phaseDescription: Add architectural elements and decorative features.

steps.title: Add Roof Structure and Details
steps.subtitle: Show roof shape and features
steps.why: Roof is a major architectural element and must be properly detailed.
instructions: Set current layer to ROOF_LINE
Draw roof outline and pitch (slope)
Show eaves, overhangs, and gutters
Add ridge lines and valley details if applicable
Show roof materials and textures
Add roof height and pitch annotations

steps.title: Add Balconies and Decks
steps.subtitle: Show exterior platforms and railings
steps.why: Balconies and decks are important architectural and safety features.
instructions: Set current layer to BALCONY
Draw balcony floor and edge details
Add railing or balustrade details
Show structural support columns if visible
Add balcony dimensions and safety details
Include access doors to balconies

steps.title: Add Mouldings and Trim
steps.subtitle: Show decorative architectural elements
steps.why: Mouldings and trim provide architectural detail and character.
instructions: Set current layer to MOULDING
Add cornice mouldings at roof line
Show window and door trim details
Add decorative bands or belt courses
Show base mouldings at foundation line
Include other decorative trim elements

phases.phaseDescription: Show exterior materials and finishes with appropriate patterns.

steps.title: Add Brick and Masonry Patterns
steps.subtitle: Show brick or masonry finishes
steps.why: Material patterns help identify exterior finishes and construction methods.
instructions: Set current layer to BRICK_PATTERN
Use HATCH with brick pattern for brick walls
Show different brick bonds if required (running bond, Flemish bond)
Add weep holes and expansion joints if required
Show brick details at corners and openings
Add brick type and color specifications

steps.title: Add Stone and Other Materials
steps.subtitle: Show stone and other exterior materials
steps.why: Different materials must be clearly distinguished for construction and aesthetic purposes.
instructions: Set current layer to STONE_PATTERN
Use HATCH with stone pattern for stone veneer
Show stone details at foundation or accent areas
Add stucco or plaster finishes using appropriate patterns
Show wood siding or cladding if used
Add material specifications and transitions

steps.title: Add Metal and Glass Features
steps.subtitle: Show modern architectural materials
steps.why: Metal and glass elements require special detailing and annotation.
instructions: Show metal panel systems or curtain walls
Add glass railing systems if used
Show metal roofing or flashing details
Add decorative metal features or ornaments
Include material specifications and finishes

phases.phaseDescription: Add dimensions, levels, and technical annotations.

steps.title: Add Vertical Dimensions
steps.subtitle: Complete height dimensioning
steps.why: Vertical dimensions are essential for construction and code compliance.
instructions: Set current layer to DIM_ELEV
Add overall building height dimensions
Dimension floor-to-floor heights
Add opening heights (doors, windows)
Show foundation depth below grade
Include roof height and ridge dimensions

steps.title: Add Horizontal Dimensions
steps.subtitle: Complete width dimensioning
steps.why: Horizontal dimensions show building width and opening locations.
instructions: Add overall building width dimensions
Dimension opening locations and widths
Show spacing between architectural features
Add projection dimensions for overhangs
Include module or grid dimensions if applicable

steps.title: Add Level Annotations and Grades
steps.subtitle: Show finished floor and grade levels
steps.why: Level annotations are critical for construction and site work.
instructions: Set current layer to TEXT_ELEV
Add finished floor level annotations (e.g., '+0.000', '+3.000')
Show existing and finished grade levels
Add ceiling height annotations if required
Include roof height and parapet details
Use standard level annotation symbols

steps.title: Add Material and Finish Annotations
steps.subtitle: Identify all exterior materials
steps.why: Material annotations are required for construction specifications and municipal approval.
instructions: Add material labels for all different finishes
Use QLEADER to point to specific material areas
Include color and finish specifications
Add manufacturer information if required
Show special treatment or coating details

phases.phaseDescription: Add site context and complete elevation details.

steps.title: Add Site Context and Surroundings
steps.subtitle: Show relationship to adjacent elements
steps.why: Site context helps reviewers understand the building's relationship to its surroundings.
instructions: Show adjacent buildings or property lines if relevant
Add existing ground contours or slopes
Show landscaping elements that affect the elevation
Add site features like retaining walls or steps
Include property line setbacks if visible

steps.title: Add Building Code Annotations
steps.subtitle: Include required code information
steps.why: Building code annotations are mandatory for municipal permit approval.
instructions: Add fire rating requirements for exterior walls
Show required egress windows and their dimensions
Include accessibility requirements for entrances
Add facade material fire ratings if required
Show required clearances from property lines

steps.title: Add North Arrow and Scale
steps.subtitle: Essential elevation elements
steps.why: North arrow and scale are mandatory for all elevation submissions.
instructions: Insert north arrow showing elevation direction (e.g., 'NORTH ELEVATION')
Add scale bar appropriate to drawing scale (1:50 or 1:100)
Include written scale notation
Place elements in non-obtrusive but visible locations

steps.title: Create Title Block and Drawing Information
steps.subtitle: Complete submission documentation
steps.why: Title block provides essential project identification and drawing information.
instructions: Create title block with project name, elevation title, scale, date
Include drawing number and elevation direction (North, South, East, West)
Add designer, checker, and approval signatures
Include municipal reference numbers and permit details
Add drawing index and related sheet references

steps.title: Final Municipal Compliance Check
steps.subtitle: Verify all requirements with field verification
steps.why: Municipalities require field-verified elevations with complete material and code compliance information.
instructions: Verify all dimensions match field measurements within tolerance
Check material annotations include fire ratings and specifications
Ensure building height complies with zoning restrictions
Confirm accessibility features are properly shown
Verify façade materials meet fire and energy codes
Prepare field measurement verification sheet
Create PDF submission with print-optimized layers
